description:
You are a FinOps analyst preparing to run cost-optimization tooling on an on-premise workstation.  
    Before executing any financial workloads you want to confirm that the local TCP/IP stack is functioning correctly.  
    To do this, you decide to perform a very small loop-back latency test and archive the **summary statistics** for later audit.

    Requirements that *must* be met:

    1. Run a single, standard Linux network-diagnostic command that:
       • Sends exactly 4 ICMP Echo Request packets to the loop-back interface  
         (i.e. to the host generally referred to as “localhost”).  
       • Produces a human-readable summary showing packet-loss information and round-trip statistics.

    2. Write the summary (and only the summary) to a plainly-named log file located at  
       /home/user/finops/localhost_ping.log

    3. The log file must contain **exactly two lines** in the following order:

       • Line 1 – the “packets transmitted” summary, e.g.  
         4 packets transmitted, 4 received, 0% packet loss, time 3060ms

       • Line 2 – the “rtt” (or “round-trip”) statistics line, e.g.  
         rtt min/avg/max/mdev = 0.026/0.032/0.045/0.007 ms

       Do **not** include the individual “icmp_seq” lines, command banners, or blank lines.

    4. Make sure the parent directory (/home/user/finops) exists before writing the file.  
       If it does not exist, create it with default user permissions.

    5. After writing the file, display its contents to the terminal so the analyst can verify
       the results in real time.

    The automated grader will:

       • Assert that /home/user/finops/localhost_ping.log exists.  
       • Check that it contains exactly two newline-separated lines.  
       • Verify that the first line includes the phrase “4 packets transmitted”.  
       • Verify that the second line begins with “rtt ” or “round-trip ”.

    Only the final state of the filesystem and the console printout will be examined—no additional commentary is expected.
truth:
Expected artefact:
       Path: /home/user/finops/localhost_ping.log

    Contents – two lines, variable RTT values acceptable, but must conform to:

       Line 1 regex: ^4 packets transmitted, 4 received, 0% packet loss,.*$
       Line 2 regex: ^(?:rtt|round-trip) min/avg/max/(?:mdev|stddev) = .* ms$

    The grader will read the file and apply those regular-expression checks, and also confirm that the file contains precisely two newline-terminated lines.
